headline: Heat peaks 97°F Saturday — protect beds before the gusts.
weather_notes: Secure e1–e4 tomato cage ties before Saturday — 33 mph gusts at 97°F can snap stems on 26-day-old transplants.
Water all beds early Saturday morning — 97°F arrives 2 days after today's Rachio run with zero rain in the forecast.
Skip supplemental watering on w2/w3 garlic this week — 97°F heat is accelerating curing and excess moisture at 41 days from harvest risks wrapper damage before the June 21 stop.
Check c2–c4 bean beds daily for emergence — day-7 seeds are mid-germination and 88–97°F heat with no rain can bake the seed zone dry.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due Sunday; 88–97°F heat through Saturday will surge lateral growth on this indeterminate variety — pinch before suckers pass 2 inches.

suggested_tasks.title: Hill remaining 3 potato_bags plants
suggested_tasks.description: Three of five sprouted plants still need hilling after the June 3 partial job — cover stems before heat-wave growth makes it harder to do cleanly.

suggested_tasks.title: Mulch e1–e4 tomato bases before Saturday
suggested_tasks.description: Apply 2-inch straw mulch around tomato bases before Saturday — bare soil under 97°F with 33 mph gusts will lose moisture rapidly.
